section_title: An agent has authority, in an emergency, to do all such acts for the
content:
189. An agent has authority, in an emergency, to do all such acts for the
authority in
purpose of protecting his principal from loss as would be done by a person of
an
emergency ordinary prudence, in his own case, under similar circumstances.
Illustrations
(a) An agent for sale may have goods repaired if it be necessary.
(b) A consigns provisions to B at Chittagong, with directions to send them
immediately to C at Dhaka. B may sell the provisions at Chittagong, if they
will not bear the journey to Dhaka without spoiling.
Sub-Agents
When agent
